name: Villacañas to La Puebla de Almoradiel – circular via the Vía Verde del Trenillo
name_original: Villacañas a La Puebla de Almoradiel – circular por el Vía Verde del Trenillo
region_intro: Touring cycling around Villacañas is characterized by its flat to undulating terrain, featuring a significant complex of wetlands and agricultural landscapes. The region is home to several lagoons, including Laguna Larga and Laguna de Peñahueca, which are part of the "Humedales de La Mancha," a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. These natural features provide a unique backdrop for cycling, with routes often passing through vineyards and olive groves. The area's elevation changes are generally gentle, making it accessible for various cycling abilities. Best touring cycling routes around Villacañas The most popular touring cycling route is La Mancha Wetlands Loop, a 38.1 miles (61.4 km) trail that takes 5 hours to complete. This difficult route explores the ecologically significant wetlands of the region. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vía Verde del Trenillo Loop, a moderate 27.0 miles (43.5 km) path. This route follows a former railway line, offering views of wetlands, vineyards, and olive groves. Local touring cyclists also love the Villacañas Lagoons and La Guardia Loop, a 41.7 miles (67.1 km) trail leading through the significant lagoons of Villacañas and La Guardia, often completed in about 5 hours 20 minutes. Touring cycling around Villacañas is defined by its extensive wetlands, traditional vineyards, and olive groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate greenways to more challenging loops. The routes in Villacañas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. More than 50 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Villacañas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of touring cycling routes are available in Villacañas?
A: Villacañas offers a diverse range of touring cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find everything from easy paths to more challenging loops. Overall, there are 28 routes, with 2 easy, 20 moderate, and 6 difficult options, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any family-friendly cycling routes in Villacañas?
A: Yes, Villacañas has several routes suitable for families, particularly those looking for a more relaxed experience. The region's generally flat to undulating terrain makes many paths accessible. Look for the 'easy' or 'moderate' rated routes, which often pass through peaceful countryside and greenways, ideal for a family outing.

Q: Can I explore the famous wetlands and lagoons by bike?
A: Absolutely! The wetlands and lagoons are a major highlight for touring cyclists in Villacañas. Routes like the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022588258/la-mancha-wetlands-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mancha Wetlands Loop&lt;/a&gt; (61.4 km) and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022690728/villacanas-lagoons-and-la-guardia-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villacañas Lagoons and La Guardia Loop&lt;/a&gt; (67.1 km) are specifically designed to showcase these ecologically significant areas, offering opportunities for birdwatching and enjoying the tranquil natural environment.

Q: What is the Vía Verde del Trenillo and what can I expect on this route?
A: The Vía Verde del Trenillo is a popular greenway built on a former railway line. It offers a moderate 43.5 km loop, known as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022692873/via-verde-del-trenillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Trenillo Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Cyclists can enjoy a scenic journey through wetlands, vineyards, and olive groves in the Toledo plain, providing a glimpse into the region's agricultural heritage.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Villacañas?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Villacañas are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022692576/villacanas-to-la-puebla-de-almoradiel-circular-via-the-via-verde-del-trenillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villacañas to La Puebla de Almoradiel – circular via the Vía Verde del Trenillo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 62.4 km loop, perfect for a day trip.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I see while cycling in Villacañas?
A: The cycling routes in Villacañas offer a rich tapestry of landscapes. You'll primarily encounter flat to undulating terrain, characterized by extensive wetlands, traditional vineyards, and olive groves. The region is part of the 'Humedales de La Mancha,' a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, so expect unique flora and diverse birdlife, especially around the numerous lagoons.

Q: What are some notable attractions or landmarks I can see near the cycling routes?
A: While cycling around Villacañas, you can explore several interesting landmarks and natural features. The region is rich in wetlands, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5421216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Grande de Villafranca&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5878870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Peñahueca&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4529824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Larga of Villacañas&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass through charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3161518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Madridejos&lt;/a&gt; or see historical buildings such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6313232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción in Tembleque&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Villacañas?
A: The touring cycling experience in Villacañas is highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. Over 50 touring cyclists have explored the varied terrain using komoot, often praising the unique wetland landscapes and the well-maintained greenways like the Vía Verde del Trenillo.

Q: Is there a route that connects different towns in the La Mancha region?
A: Yes, for those looking to explore more of the La Mancha region and its towns, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022696479/tembleque-and-la-mancha-towns-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tembleque and La Mancha Towns Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This 74.1 km route takes you through various settlements, offering a broader experience of the local culture and landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Villacañas?
A: Villacañas, located in Castilla-La Mancha, experiences hot, dry summers and mild winters. The most comfortable times for touring cycling are typically spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is generally pleasant, and you can avoid the intense heat of summer, making for a more enjoyable ride through the wetlands and agricultural areas.

Q: Are there options for parking near the cycling routes?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not provided, Villacañas is a town with various access points to its cycling network. It's generally advisable to look for public parking areas within the town or at designated starting points for popular routes like the Vía Verde del Trenillo. Planning your start point in advance can help ensure convenient parking.
tips: Villacañas (Toledo), in the heart of Toledo's La Mancha region, is a destination that combines cultural heritage, unique nature, and singular traditions.

Founded in 1343 by Prince Don Fadrique Alfonso of Castile, Master of the Order of Santiago, who granted the Charter and the Sepúlveda charter.
It was originally called La Puebla de la Isla because it was surrounded by water.
The current name honors Don Fadrique, who took refuge in these lands fleeing from Pedro I "The Cruel."

La Puebla de Almoradiel: Its origins are linked to an Iberian and Celtic fortress, inhabited by Romans, Visigoths, and, during the Muslim era, by the Almoravids.
The ancient city stood on the Cigüela River, but was abandoned in 1276 due to epidemics.
Repopulated by the Order of Santiago in the Middle Ages, it received a town charter in 1315 and the privilege of town status in 1341.
